nodes.name: install.php
nodes.summary: Assistant d'installation web en 5 étapes (prérequis, DB, schéma SQL embarqué, organisation/seed, compte admin) écrivant conf/db.php.

nodes.name: login.php
nodes.summary: Page de connexion avec protection CSRF, temporisation anti-bruteforce et création de session via authLogin.

nodes.name: set-password.php
nodes.summary: Page publique de définition de mot de passe pour nouveaux utilisateurs via un token d'invitation expirant.

nodes.name: attestation_bulk.php
nodes.summary: Generates bulk PDF donation attestations for all eligible donors in a given year by filling an FDF template and streaming a merged PDF via pdftk.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FDF (Forms Data Format) generation in PHP to fill PDF form fields without a PHP PDF library — relies on pdftk for the actual merge.

nodes.name: attestation_don.php
nodes.summary: Generates a single PDF donation attestation for a specified member and accounting entry by filling an FDF template and piping it through pdftk.

nodes.name: bootstrap.php
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap library providing DB connection setup, shared utility functions (date formatting, unquote, maxval sequence management), and the central auditLog helper used across all action handlers.

nodes.name: auth.php
nodes.summary: Core authentication library providing session-based login/logout, role-checking helpers (isAdmin, isManager, canWrite, canRead), and access guards (requireLogin, requirePasswordChange).

nodes.name: import_fields.php
nodes.summary: Source unique des champs importables du wizard CSV : libellés, liste autorisée et normalisation des valeurs (civilité).

nodes.name: actions.php
nodes.summary: Action router that dispatches incoming POST requests to the correct action handler file based on the action query parameter, enforcing authentication and write-permission guards.

nodes.name: menu.php
nodes.summary: Renders the main navigation sidebar menu with role-aware visibility, highlighting the active section, and links to all application modules.

nodes.name: donor_table.php
nodes.summary: Reusable partial rendering a Bootstrap-styled DataTable of donor rows with amounts, dates, and action links, included by multiple donor view files.

nodes.name: resources_fr.php
nodes.summary: Locale française : dictionnaire $GLOBAL des chaînes d'interface (navigation, actions, formulaires, tableau de bord).

nodes.name: authUser
nodes.summary: Reads the current authenticated user from the PHP session, returning null if not logged in.

nodes.name: isAdmin
nodes.summary: Prédicat RBAC : vrai si le rôle en session est admin.

nodes.name: isManager
nodes.summary: Prédicat RBAC : vrai pour les rôles admin ou manager.

nodes.name: canWrite
nodes.summary: Prédicat RBAC : vrai pour admin, manager ou user (droit d'écriture).

nodes.name: canRead
nodes.summary: Prédicat RBAC : vrai pour tout rôle authentifié y compris readonly.

nodes.name: requireLogin
nodes.summary: Redirects unauthenticated requests to the login page, acting as a route guard.

nodes.name: requirePasswordChange
nodes.summary: Forces users whose password has been flagged as temporary to change it before accessing the application.

nodes.name: authLogin
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user by verifying the password hash, establishing the session, and recording last_login in the database.

nodes.name: authLogout
nodes.summary: Destroys the current session and clears the session cookie to log the user out.

nodes.name: getMaxVal
nodes.summary: Retrieves (or initializes) the current value of a named sequence counter from the maxval table.

nodes.name: updateAndGetMaxVal
nodes.summary: Increments a named sequence counter in the maxval table and returns the new value.

nodes.name: formatedDateToTimeStamp
nodes.summary: Parses a locale-formatted date string (d.m.Y or d/m/Y) into a Unix timestamp, returning false on parse errors.

nodes.name: timeStampToformatedDate
nodes.summary: Converts a Unix timestamp to a locale-formatted date string (d.m.Y).

nodes.name: unquote
nodes.summary: Remplace les apostrophes typographiques par des apostrophes droites.

nodes.name: auditLog
nodes.summary: Inserts a row into the audit_log table recording the current user, action type, detail, and optionally a subject user ID.

nodes.name: importFieldLabels
nodes.summary: Retourne la map champ->libellé FR des champs importables (source unique de vérité).

nodes.name: importAllowedFields
nodes.summary: Retourne la liste des clés de champs autorisés à l'import.

nodes.name: importNormalizeSexe
nodes.summary: Normalise une civilité en texte libre vers l'enum sexe (na/f/m/hf).

nodes.name: importFieldValue
nodes.summary: Applique la normalisation par champ à une valeur CSV brute avant assignation.

nodes.name: fdf_generate
nodes.summary: Construit un document FDF (Form Data Format) pour remplir le formulaire PDF via pdftk.

nodes.name: Rôles RBAC
nodes.summary: Modèle de contrôle d'accès à 4 rôles (admin, manager, user, readonly) appliqué via des prédicats et des guards dans le routeur de vues.

nodes.name: Attestations PDF via FDF/pdftk
nodes.summary: Génération d'attestations de dons en remplissant un template AcroForm avec des données FDF (encodage UTF-16BE) exécutées par pdftk.

nodes.name: Wizard import CSV
nodes.summary: Import CSV en 3 étapes (upload/apply/resolveDuplicates) avec une source unique de champs et normalisation des valeurs.

nodes.name: Réglages d'application
nodes.summary: Configuration organisationnelle stockée en table app_settings (nom, adresse, équipes par défaut) chargée au bootstrap et utilisée partout.

nodes.name: property_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines the UserProperty class, an Active Record model for user_properties rows used by both segment membership and follow-up notes, with upsert and remove.

nodes.name: UserProperty
nodes.summary: Active Record model for user_properties entries, shared by segment membership and suivi notes, with upsert persistence using updateAndGetMaxVal.

nodes.name: auth.php
nodes.summary: Handles authentication POST actions including login, logout, and password change, validating credentials and redirecting accordingly via htmx-compatible headers.

nodes.name: compta.php
nodes.summary: Processes POST actions for the accounting (compta) module — creating, updating, and deleting financial entries with DB writes and htmx redirect responses.

nodes.name: groups.php
nodes.summary: Handles all group management POST actions: creating, updating, deleting groups, adding or removing members from groups, and managing group emails, with input validation and audit logging.

nodes.name: import.php
nodes.summary: Handler wizard import CSV/TSV 3 étapes. Garde isManager(). Actions: importUpload (parse fichier, détection délimiteur, conversion Latin-1→UTF-8, limites 5MB/5000 lignes), importApply (mapping→création, détection doublons en mémoire byEmail/byName, transaction, attribution segment/team, libère la session), importResolveDuplicates (choix ignore/fill/overwrite). Utilise import_fields.php.

nodes.name: metagroups.php
nodes.summary: Processes POST actions for meta-group management — creating, updating, and deleting meta-groups that categorize collections of groups.

nodes.name: settings.php
nodes.summary: Handles settings POST actions for application users and categories — creating, updating, and deleting app user accounts and member categories with permission checks.

nodes.name: suivi.php
nodes.summary: Processes POST actions for the suivi (follow-up/tracking) module, handling notes or log entries attached to members.

nodes.name: Wizard d'import CSV (3 étapes)
nodes.summary: Flux importUpload→importApply→importResolveDuplicates. État en $_SESSION['_import_*']. Détection délimiteur, conversion encodage, limites 5MB/5000 lignes, mapping colonnes→champs via import_fields.php, libération des lignes en fin d'importApply.

nodes.name: Détection de doublons à l'import
nodes.summary: Maps en mémoire byEmail et byName préchargées en une seule requête (users status=1). Résolution par email puis nom+prénom. Évite les SELECT par ligne. Résolution ultérieure: ignore/fill/overwrite.

nodes.name: Garde-fous d'autorisation (canWrite/isManager/isAdmin)
nodes.summary: Les handlers d'action vérifient les rôles en tête: canWrite() (compta, members, suivi), isManager() (groups, import, metagroups, types compta), isAdmin() (saveSettings, gestion app_users, anonymize/delete user). 403 sinon.

nodes.name: Journal d'audit (auditLog)
nodes.summary: Fonction auditLog($pdo, action, détail, [userId]) appelée par tous les handlers pour tracer les mutations. Table audit_log purgée via flushAuditLog.

nodes.name: users_add_form.php
nodes.summary: Formulaire de recherche et de création d'un nouveau membre (société, nom, prénom, sexe, coordonnées, note). Poste vers action=addUser. Peut pré-cocher l'ajout à un segment via fromTeam.

nodes.name: users_anonymize.php
nodes.summary: Page de confirmation d'anonymisation d'un membre ayant des écritures comptables (suppression impossible). Efface les données personnelles en conservant l'historique compta. Poste vers action=anonymizeUser.

nodes.name: users_general_data.php
nodes.summary: Onglet données générales du membre en mode lecture/édition inline avec Alpine.js (memberGeneralForm), sauvegarde via PUT /api/members/{id}. Éditeur riche Tiptap pour la note. Avertissement cotisation non payée.

nodes.name: users_history.php
nodes.summary: Vue admin de l'historique des modifications d'un membre, lue depuis audit_log et affichée en table DataTables triée par date décroissante.

nodes.name: users_inactive.php
nodes.summary: Liste des membres archivés (status=0) dans le layout des réglages, avec modal et formulaire de désarchivage. Poste vers action=reactivateUser (redirect inactiveUsers).

nodes.name: users_member_of.php
nodes.summary: Panneau d'appartenance aux segments d'un membre : pills groupés par catégorie, ajout/retrait via /api/groups/{id}/members avec toasts undo. Gère aussi le rendu serveur des actions addMembership/removeMembership.

nodes.name: users_merge.php
nodes.summary: Interface de fusion de deux fiches membres en doublon : table comparative des champs divergents (sélection A/B/both via Alpine mergeApp), choix du survivant et du sort du profil source (archiver/supprimer), dialog de confirmation. Poste vers action=mergeUsers.

nodes.name: compta_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Renders the accounting entry create/edit form with fields for amount, date, type, and description, submitted via htmx POST.

nodes.name: compta_last_entry.php
nodes.summary: Displays the most recent accounting entries with summary charts (Chart.js) and a type-legend helper, including inline edit and delete controls via htmx.

nodes.name: compta_list.php
nodes.summary: Renders the full accounting ledger list with DataTables, filtering by date range and type, with export and inline edit/delete actions via htmx.

nodes.name: suivi_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Formulaire d'édition d'une entrée de suivi (date, commentaire) stockée en user_properties parameter='suivi'. Poste vers action=updateSuivi, avec lien de suppression.

nodes.name: suivi_last_entry.php
nodes.summary: Liste globale des membres par dernière entrée de suivi, en table DataTables exportable triée par date décroissante.

nodes.name: suivi_list.php
nodes.summary: Onglet suivi d'un membre : ajout d'une entrée de suivi (date, commentaire) et liste des entrées existantes avec suppression. Poste vers action=addSuivi.

nodes.name: import_step1.php
nodes.summary: Assistant d'import — étape 1/3 : upload d'un fichier CSV/TSV (max 5 MB / 5000 lignes) avec messages d'erreur. Poste vers action=importUpload.

nodes.name: import_step2.php
nodes.summary: Assistant d'import — étape 2/3 : mapping des colonnes du fichier vers les champs membre (auto-détection), aperçu d'exemples, et choix d'ajout à un segment (auto/existant/nouveau/aucun). Poste vers action=importApply.

nodes.name: import_step3.php
nodes.summary: Assistant d'import — étape 3/3 : résumé des contacts créés et résolution des doublons détectés (ignorer/compléter/écraser par doublon). Poste vers action=importResolveDuplicates.

nodes.name: Mode view/edit Alpine inline
nodes.summary: Édition inline lecture/édition sur la même vue via Alpine.js, sauvegarde JSON par API REST sans rechargement de page.

nodes.name: Fusion de membres
nodes.summary: Résolution des doublons par fusion de deux fiches : sélection champ par champ, choix du survivant et suppression/archivage de la source, rattachement des données liées.

nodes.name: Assistant d'import 3 étapes
nodes.summary: Import CSV/TSV en trois étapes : upload → mapping colonnes + segment → résultats et résolution des doublons. Source des champs : import_fields.php.

nodes.name: Panneau segments du membre
nodes.summary: Gestion de l'appartenance aux segments via pills add/remove appelant /api/groups/{id}/members avec toasts d'annulation.

nodes.name: Graphiques compta Chart.js
nodes.summary: Visualisations Chart.js des écritures comptables : donut de répartition par type et timeline barres/ligne mensuel vs cumulé.

nodes.name: Anonymisation membre
nodes.summary: Effacement des données personnelles d'un membre disposant d'écritures comptables (suppression interdite pour traçabilité), conservant l'historique compta.

nodes.name: settings_app_users.php
nodes.summary: Renders the application user management admin panel, including a DataTable of existing app users and a create/edit form with role selection and password management, restricted to admins.

nodes.name: settings_audit_log.php
nodes.summary: Renders the audit log viewer displaying a paginated DataTable of all recorded system actions with user, action type, detail, and timestamp columns.

nodes.name: settings_categories.php
nodes.summary: Renders the member categories management screen with a list of existing categories and a create/edit form, restricted to admins.

nodes.name: settings_compta_types.php
nodes.summary: Gestion des types de compta : ajout, édition inline, choix de couleur (pastilles), réordonnancement drag-and-drop, et bascule des flags is_cotisation / is_excluded_from_donation / is_institutional. Réutilisable en mode embarqué (ctEmbedded) dans les réglages. Suppression uniquement si aucune entrée liée.

nodes.name: settings_filter_edit.php
nodes.summary: Formulaire d'édition d'un metagroup (catégorie ou segment combiné). Renommage, gestion de l'appartenance des segments : mode cases à cocher pour les segments combinés, mode boutons ajouter/retirer groupés par catégorie pour les catégories. Sauvegarde automatique via fetch (updateMetagroupTeams) avec toast et annulation. Suppression via modale.

nodes.name: settings_filters.php
nodes.summary: Liste des segments combinés (filtres, metagroup is_filter=1) accessibles depuis la navigation, avec nombre de segments membres, lien d'édition et formulaire d'ajout (addMetagroup, is_filter=1).

nodes.name: settings_general.php
nodes.summary: Conteneur principal des réglages : navigation par onglets (sidebar + select mobile) avec permissions par rôle (isManager/isAdmin) et inclusion des sous-vues (groupes, catégories, filtres, types compta, utilisateurs, journal, intégrité). Onglet Réglages avec formulaire htmx saveSettings (organisation, segments par défaut/membres). Gère la persistance de l'onglet actif et le drill-down updateTeam/updateMetagroup.

nodes.name: settings_group_edit.php
nodes.summary: Formulaire d'édition d'un segment (team) : renommage, masquage, catégorie. Liste des membres, imports (depuis d'autres segments, cotisants d'une année, donateurs par type/année/montant min avec compteurs précalculés). Section réaffecter/dissoudre : transfert vers un autre segment, suppression forcée avec modales de confirmation.

nodes.name: settings_groups.php
nodes.summary: Liste des segments (teams) groupés par catégorie avec section repliable des masqués. Ajout avec import (addTeamWithImport), renommage inline en AJAX (renameTeam), actions groupées (masquer/afficher via bulkHide/bulkShow, créer un filtre via bulkCreateMetagroup) avec barre de sélection et modale de nom. Toast d'annulation post bulk.

nodes.name: settings_integrity.php
nodes.summary: Outil admin de tests d'intégrité des données : détecte doublons (nom, email), segments masqués encore assignés (catégorie/segment combiné/membres), et problèmes de format (sum non numérique, date invalide, type_id NULL, emails mal formatés, sexe hors enum, membres sans nom, date de naissance future). Actions de correction : fusion de membres, fixComptaSum, deleteComptaEntry, liens vers édition.

nodes.name: donors_lapsed.php
nodes.summary: Renders the lapsed donors view listing contacts who donated previously but not in the current period, using the shared donor_table partial.

nodes.name: donors_loyal.php
nodes.summary: Renders the loyal donors view listing contacts who have donated consistently across multiple years, using the shared donor_table partial.

nodes.name: donors_new.php
nodes.summary: Renders the new donors view listing first-time donors for the current period, using the shared donor_table partial.

nodes.name: members_lapsed.php
nodes.summary: Renders the lapsed members view showing members whose membership has expired or who have not renewed, with DataTables and links to member profiles.

nodes.name: auth_change_password.php
nodes.summary: Renders the password change form with current/new password fields and client-side confirmation validation using Alpine.js.

nodes.name: Matrice des droits par rôle
nodes.summary: Tableau repliable dans le formulaire de création d'utilisateur mappant chaque droit (consulter, créer/modifier, importer, gérer segments, fusionner/archiver, supprimer/anonymiser, gérer comptes) aux 4 rôles (lecture seule, utilisateur, manager, admin). Reflète les gardes de auth.php.

nodes.name: Tests d'intégrité des données
nodes.summary: Ensemble de requêtes de diagnostic détectant doublons et incohérences de format/cohérence dans users, compta et metagroup, avec actions de correction ciblées (fusion, mise à 0, suppression d'écriture).

nodes.name: Gestion des segments (teams & metagroups)
nodes.summary: Fonctionnalité centrale de gestion des segments (teams) et de leur regroupement en catégories et segments combinés (metagroups) : création, renommage, masquage, catégorisation, imports ponctuels, actions groupées et sauvegarde AJAX de l'appartenance.

nodes.name: _bootstrap.php
nodes.summary: Bootstrap file included by all REST API endpoints; sets JSON response headers, initialises the PDO database connection, and provides the apiError() helper for returning standardised error responses.

nodes.name: compta.php
nodes.summary: REST API endpoint implementing full CRUD operations for accounting entries (compta table), including date-range filtering on list, audit logging on mutations, and Compta class integration for persistence.

nodes.name: compta-types.php
nodes.summary: REST API endpoint that returns the list of accounting types (compta_type table) as JSON; supports GET only and enforces read access control.

nodes.name: groups.php
nodes.summary: REST API endpoint implementing full CRUD for segments (teams) plus member-membership management, with manager-level access control and audit logging on all mutations.

nodes.name: suivi.php
nodes.summary: REST API endpoint implementing full CRUD for follow-up notes (user_properties suivi entries) with member ownership checks and audit logging.

nodes.name: api/.htaccess
nodes.summary: Règles Apache RewriteRule mappant les routes REST propres (/members/{id}/groups, /compta/{id}, /groups/{id}/members, etc.) vers les scripts PHP avec id et sub en query.

nodes.name: tools/fix_encoding.php
nodes.summary: Outil CLI one-shot de réparation d'encodage (mojibake CP1252/UTF-8) sur colonnes whitelistées de users. Support --dry-run et --column. À supprimer après usage.

nodes.name: tools/guest2010.php
nodes.summary: Ancien script d'import ponctuel lisant 2010.txt (TSV) et affichant les lignes en table HTML ; le code de création User/addMembership est commenté. Legacy/obsolète.

nodes.name: tools/import.php
nodes.summary: Quasi-doublon de guest2010.php : import legacy depuis 2010.txt, code de création de contacts commenté. Legacy/obsolète (import moderne = includes/actions/import.php).

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: Schéma complet MariaDB, idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), charset utf8mb4. Exécuté via /install.php. Contient 9 tables.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Membres : identité, coordonnées, sexe, birthday/dates en int timestamp, status TINYINT (1=actif). Index status, lastname, firstname.

nodes.name: team
nodes.summary: Groupes : id, name, hidden. Adhésion stockée séparément dans user_properties.

nodes.name: user_properties
nodes.summary: Propriétés génériques par membre : parameter (ex 'team_{id}', 'suivi'), value, date. Sert adhésions groupes et notes de suivi.

nodes.name: metagroup
nodes.summary: Filtres intelligents / catégories : id, name, teamid, is_filter, sort_order. Auto-jointure pour résoudre la catégorie d'une team.

nodes.name: compta_type
nodes.summary: Types comptables : label, color, sort_order, flags is_cotisation / is_excluded_from_donation / is_institutional.

nodes.name: compta
nodes.summary: Écritures comptables : user_id, date (int ts), libele, sum (varchar), quittance, type_id, wants_attestation. Index user_id/date/type_id.

nodes.name: maxval
nodes.summary: Aide à l'auto-incrément : parameter → value. Utilisé par les séquences custom (ex UserProperty).

nodes.name: app_settings
nodes.summary: Configuration applicative clé/valeur (ex membre_team, member_no_coti_team).

nodes.name: app_users
nodes.summary: Utilisateurs d'authentification : username unique, password_hash, role enum(admin/manager/user/readonly), reset_token, force_password_change.

nodes.name: audit_log
nodes.summary: Journal d'activité : app_user_id, username, action, detail, subject_user_id, created_at. Alimenté par auditLog() dans tous les endpoints d'écriture.

nodes.name: php (apache)
nodes.summary: Service PHP 8.2/Apache buildé depuis Dockerfile, port 8080:80, volumes html/conf/logs, variables DB (mariadb/members). Dépend de mariadb healthy.

nodes.name: mariadb
nodes.summary: MariaDB 11, base members, healthcheck ping, initdb monté depuis docker/initdb, volume persistant mariadb_data.

nodes.name: adminer
nodes.summary: Adminer (UI DB) sur port 8082:8080, dépend de mariadb.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Stack Docker local : php + mariadb + adminer, volumes et variables d'environnement DB.

nodes.name: docker-compose.test.yml
nodes.summary: Override compose pour tests : force DB_NAME=members_test sur le service php.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Image php:8.2-apache : extensions pdo/pdo_mysql, pdftk-java, mods Apache (rewrite/headers/expires/deflate/brotli), apache.conf, error_reporting sans E_NOTICE, dossiers logs/conf.

nodes.name: E2E Tests
nodes.summary: CI GitHub Actions sur push main / PR : monte la stack compose test, attend /login.php, installe Node 24 + Playwright chromium, reset DB via tests/fixtures/reset-db.sh, exécute Playwright, upload rapport si échec.

nodes.name: GET /api/compta
nodes.summary: Liste des écritures d'un membre (memberId requis, year optionnel) (canRead).

nodes.name: GET /api/compta/{id}
nodes.summary: Détail d'une écriture (canRead), 404 si absente.

nodes.name: POST /api/compta
nodes.summary: Crée une écriture (canWrite) : valide memberId/typeId/date/amount, audit addCompta, 201.

nodes.name: PUT /api/compta/{id}
nodes.summary: Met à jour une écriture (canWrite), valide typeId, audit updateCompta.

nodes.name: DELETE /api/compta/{id}
nodes.summary: Supprime une écriture (canWrite), audit deleteCompta, 204.

nodes.name: GET /api/compta-types
nodes.summary: Liste lecture seule des types comptables (auth requise via bootstrap, pas de guard rôle spécifique).

nodes.name: GET /api/groups
nodes.summary: Liste des groupes avec nb membres actifs et catégorie.

nodes.name: GET /api/groups/{id}
nodes.summary: Détail d'un groupe avec nb membres et catégorie, 404 si absent.

nodes.name: GET /api/groups/{id}/members
nodes.summary: Membres actifs d'un groupe.

nodes.name: POST /api/groups
nodes.summary: Crée un groupe (isManager), name requis, audit addTeam, 201.

nodes.name: PUT /api/groups/{id}
nodes.summary: Renomme / bascule hidden (isManager), audit renameTeam.

nodes.name: DELETE /api/groups/{id}
nodes.summary: Supprime un groupe (isManager) seulement s'il est vide (409 sinon), audit deleteTeam, 204.

nodes.name: POST /api/groups/{id}/members
nodes.summary: Ajoute un membre au groupe (isManager) via INSERT IGNORE user_properties, audit addMembership, 204.

nodes.name: DELETE /api/groups/{id}/members
nodes.summary: Retire un membre du groupe (isManager), audit removeMembership, 204.

nodes.name: GET /api/suivi
nodes.summary: Liste des notes de suivi d'un membre (memberId requis) (canRead).

nodes.name: GET /api/suivi/{id}
nodes.summary: Détail d'une note de suivi (canRead), 404 si absente.

nodes.name: POST /api/suivi
nodes.summary: Crée une note (canWrite) : memberId/date/note requis, audit addSuivi, 201.

nodes.name: PUT /api/suivi/{id}
nodes.summary: Met à jour une note (canWrite), audit updateSuivi.

nodes.name: DELETE /api/suivi/{id}
nodes.summary: Supprime une note (canWrite), audit deleteSuivi, 204.

nodes.name: api.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E Playwright des endpoints REST JSON (/api/members, /api/compta-types, /api/compta, /api/groups, /api/suivi) : garde d'authentification 401 sans session, format des réponses, listes et filtres.

nodes.name: app-users.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E de la gestion des utilisateurs applicatifs (vue manageAppUsers) : liste, création via modal, suppression d'un compte utilisateur.

nodes.name: auth.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E d'authentification (sans session pré-injectée) : login mauvais mot de passe, login correct redirige vers la liste, logout redirige vers la page de connexion.

nodes.name: change-password.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E du flux de changement de mot de passe (vue changePassword) via POST fetch et vérification de la redirection.

nodes.name: compta-types.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E des types comptables dans Réglages (onglet compta) : navigation, ajout (addComptaType) et suppression.

nodes.name: compta.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E des écritures comptables d'un membre : consultation, ajout, édition et suppression via htmx.

nodes.name: groups.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E de la gestion des groupes/segments (team) dans Réglages : liste, création (addTeamWithImport), renommage, suppression.

nodes.name: inactive-members.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E des membres archivés : désactivation (status=0), listing des inactifs et réactivation.

nodes.name: members.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E du CRUD membres : liste, recherche par nom, ajout, édition, suppression.

nodes.name: merge-users.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E du flux de fusion de deux membres (doublons) : création de deux membres temporaires puis fusion.

nodes.name: metagroups.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E des métagroupes / segments combinés (filtres) dans Réglages : création (addMetagroup), renommage, suppression.

nodes.name: resume.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E de la vue résumé/statistiques des dons : cartes KPI, filtre par année et par montant minimum.

nodes.name: roles.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E du contrôle d'accès par rôle (readonly/user/manager/admin) : visibilité des actions dans l'UI et blocage serveur (HTTP 403) des appels API forcés.

nodes.name: settings.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E de la page Réglages : navigation entre onglets (général, groupes, catégories, compta) et sauvegarde des paramètres.

nodes.name: suivi.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E du CRUD des notes de suivi d'un membre : consultation, ajout, édition, suppression.

nodes.name: views.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Tests E2E des vues de reporting en lecture seule : journal d'audit, historique utilisateur, dernières entrées compta/suivi.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Présentation générale de MemberBase (gestion membres/groupes/cotisations/dons en PHP 8.2), table des liens vers la documentation et guide d'installation.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Historique des versions ; v3.5.4 : terminologie « Segment », import CSV/TSV en 3 étapes, champ email_alt, tests d'intégrité étendus, gardes de rôle API.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Guide de contribution : mise en place de l'environnement Docker, stack (PHP 8.2, Bootstrap, htmx, Alpine, pas de build), conventions et bonnes pratiques.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Charte de design « Casa Membres » : palette de couleurs, ton sobre d'outil admin interne et directives visuelles.

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Définition produit : utilisateurs (collaborateurs internes, 4 rôles), objectif (gestion des membres/cotisations/dons, exports, API REST) et critères de succès.

nodes.name: MIGRATION_PROD.md
nodes.summary: Liste des migrations manuelles à appliquer en production par version (ex. v3.5.4 : ajout de la colonne email_alt à la table users).

nodes.name: BootstrapXL.css
nodes.summary: Single media query that widens Bootstrap's .container to 1570px on screens wider than 1600px, extending the default Bootstrap grid for extra-large displays.

nodes.name: custom.css
nodes.summary: Primary application stylesheet for the MemberBase app: defines a CSS custom-property design token palette (brand blues, ink, danger), overrides Bootstrap 5 components (buttons, navbar, tables, dropdowns), and provides application-specific UI classes for member pills, group lists, merge/diff views, DataTables, modals, htmx loading states, and print styles.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a comprehensive :root custom property palette (--ca-* tokens) used as the single source of truth for brand colors throughout the application.

nodes.name: docs.css
nodes.summary: Bootstrap documentation stylesheet (1110 lines) providing scaffolding for the project's internal documentation pages: navigation, callouts, code snippet highlighting, side navigation, grid examples, and miscellaneous Bootstrap-docs-era styles.
nodes.languageNotes: Appears to be the canonical Bootstrap 3 docs stylesheet, adapted or bundled for the project's internal documentation section.

nodes.name: navbar-fixed-top.css
nodes.summary: Small stylesheet overriding the Bootstrap docs navigation bar (.bs-docs-nav) to apply the project's brand blue (#6088c1) background and white link colors with an orange hover accent.

nodes.name: bug_report.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for bug reports, with structured fields for environment, steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behaviour, and optional screenshots.

nodes.name: feature_request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue template for feature requests, collecting problem description, proposed solution, and alternatives considered.

nodes.name: pull_request_template.md
nodes.summary: Pull request template with sections for what the PR does, motivation, changes, testing steps, and a review checklist.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Configuration for the Impeccable UI review tool, specifying the detector mode used to evaluate front-end interfaces in this project.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore rules for the Understand-Anything knowledge graph scanner, excluding generated files, vendor directories, and build artefacts from analysis.

nodes.name: admin.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive administrator guide covering installation via web wizard and Docker, Apache configuration, user account management, security hardening, REST API usage, backup procedures, CI/CD pipeline, audit logs, and schema migrations for MemberBase.

nodes.name: user.md
nodes.summary: End-user guide covering login, member list navigation, CSV import wizard, member record editing, accounting entries, follow-up notes, donation attestations, segment management, and role permissions for MemberBase.

nodes.name: apache.conf
nodes.summary: Apache VirtualHost configuration for the Docker development environment, enabling mod_rewrite URL routing for the REST API endpoints under /api/ and serving the PHP application from /var/www/html.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses RewriteRule to route /api/<resource>/<id> to api/<resource>.php?id=<id>, implementing clean REST URLs without a framework router.

nodes.name: 0F5B592E883E10E5F744B70B861AE02B.txt
nodes.summary: Let's Encrypt domain ownership verification token served from the web root for TLS certificate provisioning.

nodes.name: apiError
nodes.summary: Emits a JSON error response with the given HTTP status code and message, then terminates execution.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: Apache .htaccess for the api/ directory; blocks direct file access and enforces mod_rewrite routing so clean REST URLs resolve to the correct PHP handler.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/compta — lists accounting entries with optional member_id and date-range filters, returning paginated JSON.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/compta — validates required fields, persists a new accounting entry via the Compta class, and writes an audit log entry.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: Handles PUT /api/compta/:id — loads an existing entry, applies changed fields, saves, and appends an audit log entry.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: Handles DELETE /api/compta/:id — logs the deletion with amount details to the audit log and removes the entry via the Compta class.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: Lists all segments with member counts and optional metagroup hierarchy info, returning structured JSON.

nodes.name: handleListMembers
nodes.summary: Lists all members belonging to a given segment, verifying the segment exists before querying.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: Creates a new segment, requiring manager role, then logs the creation to the audit log.

nodes.name: handleAddMember
nodes.summary: Adds a member to a segment by inserting a user_properties row, with existence checks for both segment and member, plus audit logging.

nodes.name: handleRemoveMember
nodes.summary: Removes a member from a segment by deleting the user_properties row and recording the action in the audit log.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: Apache .htaccess that denies direct HTTP access to the classes/ directory, ensuring PHP class files can only be included server-side.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: Apache .htaccess that denies direct HTTP access to the includes/ directory, protecting server-side PHP include files.

nodes.name: members.php
nodes.summary: Processes member POST actions including create, update, delete, and merge (duplicate resolution), with audit logging, input validation, and htmx-compatible redirects.

nodes.name: _lec_type_swatch
nodes.summary: Renders a colored badge/swatch for an accounting entry type, with optional full-label or initial character display.

nodes.name: user_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines the User domain class: hydration/lookup of members, getters/setters for all contact fields, cotisation/donation payment checks against the compta table, team membership management, persistence (save/remove), and the static listWithFilters() query that powers filtered member listings.
nodes.languageNotes: Active-record style PHP class using a global PDO handle; listWithFilters() delegates virtual-filter detection to MemberFilter and metagroup expansion to Metagroup::teamIds().

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: Active-record model for a member (users table): lookups by id/email, full field accessors, compta-based payment predicates, team membership add/remove, and persistence with prepared statements.

nodes.name: hydrateFromRow
nodes.summary: Copies a database row object into the User instance's typed properties, including the status flag.

nodes.name: lookupUser
nodes.summary: Loads a member by primary key via prepared statement and hydrates the instance; returns whether a row was found.

nodes.name: lookupUserByEmail
nodes.summary: Loads a member by email address and hydrates the instance, used for duplicate detection and login-style lookups.

nodes.name: isCotisationPayed
nodes.summary: Checks whether the member has a membership-fee (cotisation) compta entry within the given calendar year using firstComptaDate().

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the member: UPDATE when an id exists, otherwise INSERT with creation/modification timestamps, capturing the new id via lastInsertId().

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes the member and cascades manual cleanup of user_properties and compta rows.

nodes.name: listWithFilters
nodes.summary: Static query builder returning active members filtered by team, metagroup (expanded via Metagroup::teamIds), search text, and virtual filters detected through MemberFilter::isVirtual; single source for the members list view and REST API.

nodes.name: team_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines the Team domain class: lookup and persistence of teams, metagroup membership management, usage checks before deletion, plus static helpers nameById() and listForDropdown() that feed navigation selects with category grouping and member counts.

nodes.name: Team
nodes.summary: Active-record model for the team table with hidden-flag support, metagroup membership operations, and static list/name helpers for UI dropdowns.

nodes.name: lookupTeam
nodes.summary: Loads a team by id via prepared statement and populates id, name, and hidden fields.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the team as UPDATE or INSERT depending on whether an id is set, capturing the new id on insert.

nodes.name: nameById
nodes.summary: Static helper returning a team's name for a given id, or null when not found; avoids instantiating a full Team object for display purposes.

nodes.name: listForDropdown
nodes.summary: Static query returning all visible teams with their metagroup category, sort order, and member counts, ordered for grouped dropdown rendering.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes the team only when isUsed() reports no remaining member associations, guarding referential integrity.

nodes.name: metagroup_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines the Metagroup domain class grouping teams into named categories/filters, with lookup, persistence, guarded deletion, and static helpers filterList(), teamNames(), and teamIds() consumed by member listing filters.

nodes.name: Metagroup
nodes.summary: Active-record model for the metagroup table (a group of team ids sharing one id/name), with static helpers exposing filter metagroups and their team memberships.

nodes.name: lookupMetagroup
nodes.summary: Loads a metagroup's id and name by primary key via prepared statement.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the metagroup name; on insert, allocates the next id via updateAndGetMaxVal() because metagroup ids are shared across rows rather than auto-incremented.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a manual max-id allocation (updateAndGetMaxVal) instead of AUTO_INCREMENT because one metagroup id spans multiple (id, teamid) rows.

nodes.name: filterList
nodes.summary: Static query returning metagroups flagged is_filter=1 that have at least one team, used to populate the member-list filter dropdown.

nodes.name: teamNames
nodes.summary: Static helper returning the names of all teams belonging to a metagroup, for display in admin views.

nodes.name: teamIds
nodes.summary: Static helper returning the integer team ids of a metagroup, used by User::listWithFilters to expand a metagroup filter into a team IN-clause.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes the metagroup only when isUsed() confirms no team rows remain attached to it.

nodes.name: compta_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines the Compta domain class for accounting entries (cotisations, donations) tied to members, with lookup, accessors, persistence, and static aggregation helpers typesByUser() and activitySummaryByUser() for listings and yearly reports.

nodes.name: Compta
nodes.summary: Active-record model for the compta table (user_id, type, date, amount, receipt, attestation flag) plus static batch aggregations grouped by member.

nodes.name: lookupCompta
nodes.summary: Loads an accounting entry by id and populates all fields including quittance and wants_attestation.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the accounting entry as UPDATE or INSERT with all seven fields bound via prepared statements.

nodes.name: typesByUser
nodes.summary: Static batch query returning the distinct compta types per member for a set of user ids in a single IN-clause query, avoiding per-row SELECTs in listings.

nodes.name: activitySummaryByUser
nodes.summary: Static aggregation returning per-member accounting activity (counts/sums) for a given calendar year, used for annual reporting views.

nodes.name: member_filter_class.php
nodes.summary: Defines MemberFilter, the single source of truth for virtual member filters (e.g. no-cotisation, institutional donors): isVirtual() detects virtual filter ids and resolveIds() resolves each filter to concrete member ids; consumed by both the members list view and the REST API.
nodes.languageNotes: All-static PHP class taking the PDO handle and app settings as parameters instead of globals, making it reusable from both web views and the REST API.

nodes.name: MemberFilter
nodes.summary: Static utility class mapping virtual filter ids to member-id sets: cotisation status, donation windows, and institutional-type entries, with noCotiMembers() as a shared sub-query.

nodes.name: isVirtual
nodes.summary: Returns whether a team/filter id belongs to the reserved set of virtual filters that require resolveIds() instead of a plain team join.

nodes.name: resolveIds
nodes.summary: Resolves a virtual filter id into the concrete list of member ids by running the matching SQL (no-cotisation, payment-year windows, institutional donor types), parameterized by year and app settings.

nodes.name: noCotiMembers
nodes.summary: Shared query returning active members without any cotisation entry in the configured period, reused by two branches of resolveIds().

nodes.name: views.php
nodes.summary: Declarative route table ($UA_VIEW_ROUTES) that dispatches include-based sub-views: each view name maps to [file, auth guard, request overrides, view var]. A view absent from the table renders 'Vue introuvable', forcing every new view to declare its guard (canWrite/isManager/isAdmin) explicitly.
nodes.languageNotes: Pattern table + include dynamique : le routeur applique le guard avant include, injecte des overrides dans $_REQUEST et peut forcer la variable $view consommée par les vues partagées.

nodes.name: users_list.php
nodes.summary: Member list view with live AJAX search, team/metagroup filters, year selector, and column display. Data access is fully delegated to the class layer: User::listWithFilters() builds and runs the query, MemberFilter::resolveIds() expands virtual filters, Compta::typesByUser()/activitySummaryByUser() enrich rows, Team::listForDropdown() and Metagroup::filterList() feed the filter dropdowns.
nodes.languageNotes: Vue PHP/JS hybride : la moitié du fichier est du JavaScript inline (recherche AJAX avec AbortController, pushState, surlignage des termes) rendu par PHP.

nodes.name: users_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Shared member edit view: loads a User by id, shows badges and a mini-dashboard, then renders tabbed sections by including partials (general data, memberships, compta list, suivi list, history) selected via the $view variable set by the router.
nodes.languageNotes: Vue partagée par plusieurs routes (updateUser, generalData, compta, suivi, userHistory) : la variable $view pilotée par le routeur sélectionne l'onglet actif.

nodes.name: donors_summary.php
nodes.summary: Donor dashboard ('resume' view) summarizing members, donations and attestation figures per year, with a minimum-sum threshold selector, an includeAttestation toggle, and a Chart.js pie chart. Aggregations run as direct SQL on $pdo rather than through the class layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Le filtre attestation est appliqué dans le SUM SQL tandis que toutes les lignes restent chargées, pour que le toggle côté client reste cohérent.

nodes.name: users_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog for member disposal: offers a radio choice between archiving (deactivate, keeps history) and permanent deletion, then posts action=deleteOrDeactivateUser. Route-guarded isAdmin.

nodes.name: users_deactivate_legacy.php
nodes.summary: Legacy direct-link handler that deactivates a member (users.status=0) instead of deleting for safety, writes an audit log entry, and redirects (HX-Location for htmx requests). Route-guarded isAdmin.

nodes.name: compta_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog before deleting an accounting entry: loads the Compta record and displays date, label and amount, with cancel/delete links. Route-guarded canWrite.

nodes.name: compta_delete_do.php
nodes.summary: Delete executor for an accounting entry: resolves the member name for the audit log, calls auditLog('deleteCompta'), removes the Compta record, then re-renders the member's compta tab by including users_edit_form.php with $view='compta'.

nodes.name: suivi_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog before deleting a follow-up (suivi) entry: loads the UserProperty record and displays its date and content, with cancel/delete links. Route-guarded canWrite.

nodes.name: suivi_delete_do.php
nodes.summary: Delete executor for a follow-up (suivi) entry: resolves the member name for the audit log, calls auditLog('deleteSuivi'), removes the UserProperty record, then re-renders the member's suivi tab by including users_edit_form.php with $view='suivi'.

nodes.name: index.php
nodes.summary: Front-controller entry point of the member management app: bootstraps the session, dispatches views/actions, and renders the HTML shell that loads js/app.js and js/tiptap-editor.js with filemtime cache-busting.
nodes.languageNotes: After refactor #57/#58 it no longer contains inline JS; behavior lives in the included JS assets.

nodes.name: getMicroTime
nodes.summary: Returns the current time as a float with microsecond precision, used for simple page timing.

nodes.name: members.php
nodes.summary: REST API endpoint for members: list (with pagination, search, group and virtual filters), get, create, update, soft-delete, and group lookup, with permission checks and audit logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Soft-delete sets users.status=0; listings must filter status=1. Virtual filters delegate ID resolution to the shared MemberFilter class.

nodes.name: memberFieldsForDiff
nodes.summary: Snapshots a member's editable fields into an associative array used to compute before/after diffs for audit logging.

nodes.name: memberToArray
nodes.summary: Serializes a User object to the JSON-ready array shape returned by the API, including formatted birth, creation and modification dates.

nodes.name: requestBody
nodes.summary: Reads and JSON-decodes the raw request body, returning an apiError when the payload is not a valid JSON object.

nodes.name: applyFields
nodes.summary: Applies whitelisted request body fields onto a User object via its setters, normalizing sexe values and parsing birthday dates.

nodes.name: emitMemberList
nodes.summary: Builds and emits the paginated JSON member-list response, optionally enriching rows with member types and per-user group names.

nodes.name: handleVirtualFilter
nodes.summary: Handles virtual filter listings (e.g. all active members or named filters) by delegating ID resolution to MemberFilter::resolveIds, then fetching and emitting the matching page of members.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: GET collection endpoint: enforces read permission, parses search/pagination/group/virtual-filter parameters, runs the count and page queries against active users, and emits the member list.

nodes.name: handleGet
nodes.summary: GET single-member endpoint: enforces read permission, looks up the user by id, and returns its JSON representation or a 404 apiError.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: POST endpoint: enforces write permission, validates required name fields, applies body fields to a new User, saves it, writes an audit log entry, and returns 201 with the created member.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: PUT/PATCH endpoint: enforces write permission, applies body fields to the existing member, computes a before/after field diff, audit-logs the changes, and returns the updated member.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: DELETE endpoint: soft-deletes a member by setting users.status=0 (admin-only hard remove supported), audit-logging non-empty data before deletion, and returns 204.

nodes.name: handleGetGroups
nodes.summary: GET member-groups endpoint: verifies the member exists, then returns the list of groups the member belongs to with membership metadata as JSON.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: Global client-side behavior extracted from index.php: dirty-form guard with exclusion rules (markDirty), jQuery plugin initialization via casaInit (datepickers, DataTables, data-href rows), htmx post-swap cleanup, and save/undo toasts.
nodes.languageNotes: Works with the window.__dirtyOverride convention: inline navigation code must set it before changing window.location to bypass the beforeunload guard.

nodes.name: casaInit
nodes.summary: Initializes jQuery plugins within a DOM root: datetimepickers on .datepicker inputs, data-href table rows, and DataTable teardown/re-init, so widgets survive htmx content swaps.

nodes.name: tiptap-editor.js
nodes.summary: ES module wrapping the TipTap rich text editor: initTiptap mounts the editor and wires toolbar buttons (bold, italic, lists, undo/redo), updateToolbar syncs button active states, and destroyTiptap tears the instance down.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses TipTap's chained command API (editor.chain().focus()...run()) and stores the editor instance on the DOM element (el._tt) for later destruction.

nodes.name: initTiptap
nodes.summary: Mounts a TipTap editor on the target element, syncs its HTML content back to a hidden field on update, and binds toolbar buttons to formatting commands.

nodes.name: updateToolbar
nodes.summary: Toggles the active CSS state of toolbar buttons based on the editor's current marks (bold, italic, bullet/ordered list).

nodes.name: destroyTiptap
nodes.summary: Destroys the TipTap editor instance stored on the DOM element, freeing it before content is swapped out.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Developer notes for AI-assisted work on the project: commit identity rules, stack summary (PHP 8.2 / MariaDB / Bootstrap 5.3 / htmx), the dirty-form guard convention (__dirtyOverride, data-no-dirty), HX-Location redirect pattern, CSV import wizard flow, and the users.status filtering rule.

nodes.name: ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Onboarding guide for new developers covering local Docker setup, project layout, key entry files, request flow, and pointers to the architecture and API references.

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive REST API reference for the member management endpoints under html/api/, documenting authentication, parameters, virtual filters, response shapes, and error codes across roughly 15 sections.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture reference describing the front-controller flow through index.php, the route table and access-control guards, the MemberFilter class shared by views and API, the htmx/Alpine.js client layer in app.js, and the database schema conventions.

nodes.name: filter-parity.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test locking view/API parity of virtual member filters: asserts the members list view and the JSON API return the same result sets for each virtual filter, guarding the shared MemberFilter class against divergence between its two consumers.

nodes.name: route-guards.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test of the route table's access-control guards: verifies per-role view authorization and includes regression tests for two previously fixed access-control vulnerabilities.

nodes.name: dirty-guard.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test of the global dirty-form guard in app.js: verifies that form edits trigger the unsaved-changes prompt and that data-no-dirty elements and __dirtyOverride navigation bypass it.

nodes.name: mobile-roles.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test of the mobile menu block: opens the app in a mobile viewport per role and asserts the mobile navigation shows only entries permitted for that role.

nodes.name: openMobileAs
nodes.summary: Test helper that loads a stored authentication state for a given role, sets a mobile viewport, and navigates to the app before opening the mobile menu.

layers.name: Point d'entrée & pages racine
layers.description: Application entry points: the main dispatcher (index.php), authentication pages (login, password setup), and standalone PDF attestation generators that bootstrap the full stack and coordinate routing.

layers.name: Bibliothèque cœur
layers.description: Core library providing database bootstrapping, shared utilities (date formatting, auditLog, maxval sequences), authentication functions (login/logout, role guards), and the CSV import field registry used across the application.

layers.name: Routage
layers.description: Front-controller routers that dispatch incoming POST requests to action handlers and GET requests to view templates, enforcing authentication and role-based access on every inbound request.

layers.name: Vues
layers.description: Server-rendered PHP view templates covering member CRUD forms, accounting and follow-up tabs, import wizard steps, settings panels, donor analytics dashboards, and reusable partials (navigation sidebar, donor table).

layers.name: Concepts transverses
layers.description: Cross-cutting frontend assets: the French locale string dictionary used throughout all views, and the custom CSS stylesheets defining the application's design token palette, Bootstrap overrides, and documentation site styles.

layers.name: Classes de domaine
layers.description: Active Record domain model classes for the five core entities: User (member with 20+ properties), Team (segment/group), Compta (accounting entry), Metagroup (segment category/filter), and UserProperty (generic key-value store for memberships and follow-up notes).

layers.name: Handlers d'actions (POST)
layers.description: Server-side POST action handlers for members, authentication, accounting entries, groups, metagroups, settings, follow-up notes, and the 3-step CSV import wizard — each validating input, calling domain classes, and writing audit log entries.

layers.name: API REST
layers.description: JSON REST API layer with clean URL routing via .htaccess: a shared bootstrap initializer, five resource endpoint files (members, compta, compta-types, groups, suivi), and the 32 endpoint descriptors defining the full CRUD surface consumed by Alpine.js views.

layers.name: Outils CLI
layers.description: One-shot CLI maintenance scripts: a UTF-8/CP1252 encoding repair tool with dry-run support, and two legacy import scripts from the 2010 data migration (now obsolete).

layers.name: Schéma base de données
layers.description: Idempotent MariaDB schema (utf8mb4) defining the nine application tables — users, team, user_properties, metagroup, compta, compta_type, maxval, app_settings, app_users, and audit_log — executed during installation.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Container and server configuration: Dockerfile (PHP 8.2/Apache with pdftk), docker-compose stacks for development and test, Apache VirtualHost config for mod_rewrite REST routing, directory-level .htaccess protection files, and GitHub issue templates.

layers.name: Tests E2E
layers.description: Playwright end-to-end test suite covering all major features (authentication, members CRUD, groups, accounting, import wizard, roles/RBAC, merge, settings) plus the GitHub Actions CI workflow that provisions the test Docker stack and runs the full suite on push.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation covering product definition, architecture, REST API reference, admin and user guides, developer onboarding, design charter, changelog, migration runbooks, contribution guide, and GitHub PR/issue templates.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md gives the full picture of MemberBase: a PHP 8.2 web application built for Casa Alianza Suisse to manage members, groups, contributions and donations. It introduces the feature set (paginated lists, CSV import wizard, accounting, PDF attestations, role-based access), the tech stack (Bootstrap 5.3, htmx 2.0.4, Alpine.js, MariaDB), and links to the architecture and API documentation. Starting here gives you the vocabulary and mental model you will need for every step that follows.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: html/index.php is the single front-controller for the entire application. On every request it authenticates the user via auth.php, loads shared dependencies through bootstrap.php, then delegates to either the view router or the action router depending on whether the request is a GET (page render) or a POST (form submission). When htmx triggers a partial request, the same file detects the HX-Request header and returns only the requested fragment rather than the full page shell.
tour.languageLesson: PHP front-controller pattern: one file receives all HTTP requests, handles cross-cutting concerns (auth, session, DB), and dispatches to feature-specific handlers. This avoids duplicating bootstrap logic across dozens of endpoint files and makes it straightforward to add global middleware such as the dirty-form guard or audit logging.

tour.title: Core Library: Auth & Bootstrap
tour.description: Two libraries are loaded on every single request, making them the most imported files in the codebase (fan-in 14 and 16 respectively). auth.php provides session-based login/logout, password hashing, and the role-checking helpers -- isAdmin(), isManager(), canWrite(), canRead() -- that gate every feature. bootstrap.php opens the PDO database connection, provides shared utilities such as date formatting and the auditLog() function, and sets up the locale. Every view, action handler, and API endpoint depends on these two files.
tour.languageLesson: PHP session-based authentication stores the authenticated user ID and role in $_SESSION. The role-checking helpers (isAdmin, canWrite, etc.) are thin wrappers that read from the session and centralise the access-control logic so individual feature files need not repeat it. The app_users table (Step 6) backs these sessions.

tour.title: Routing Layer
tour.description: Two router files sit between index.php and the rest of the application. views.php (fan-out 21, the highest in the codebase) resolves the ?view= query parameter to the correct partial file and enforces authentication and role checks before including it. actions.php (fan-out 16) does the same for POST submissions, mapping the ?action= parameter to the correct handler. This clean separation means adding a new feature requires only one new view partial, one new action handler, and a single line registration in each router.

tour.title: Domain Model: Active Record Classes
tour.description: The domain logic lives in four Active Record classes under html/classes/. User is the central model with 20+ properties covering identity, contact details, team membership and financial data. Team represents a segment (a named group of members), Compta represents an accounting entry (donation or contribution), and UserProperty is the generic key-value row that backs both segment membership and follow-up notes. Each class exposes find(), save(), and delete() methods that map directly to MariaDB queries, making the data model the foundation every view and action handler builds on.
tour.languageLesson: Active Record is a pattern where each class corresponds to one database table and instances map to rows. Methods like save() issue INSERT or UPDATE automatically based on whether the row already exists. This project implements the pattern manually (no ORM framework), which keeps dependencies minimal but means the DB schema and class properties must stay in sync by convention.

tour.title: Database Schema
tour.description: The full MariaDB schema is an idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script covering 9 tables. The three central tables are users (members with a status TINYINT flag: 1=active, 0=archived), user_properties (a generic key-value store that holds both segment membership via parameter='team_{id}' rows and follow-up notes via parameter='suivi'), and compta (accounting entries linked to a user and a compta_type). The audit_log table -- the most heavily depended-on table in the codebase (fan-in 15) -- records every mutation for compliance and traceability. The app_users table stores application user accounts (username, password_hash, role enum) that back the session authentication from Step 3.
tour.languageLesson: Storing segment membership in a generic user_properties table (parameter='team_{id}', value='1') rather than a dedicated join table keeps the schema flexible: new segment types require no migration. The trade-off is that queries must filter by parameter prefix, which is why the index on (user_id, parameter) is critical for performance on large member lists.

tour.title: Action Handlers
tour.description: Action handlers under html/includes/actions/ are the POST-side of every feature. auth.php handles login, logout, and password changes. import.php implements the 3-step CSV/TSV wizard -- upload and parse, preview and map columns, resolve duplicates -- wrapping member creation in a database transaction to ensure consistency. groups.php manages creating, updating, and deleting segments and their memberships. compta.php handles accounting entry creation, editing, and deletion with DB write-back. Each handler validates access using the role helpers from Step 3 and issues an HX-Location redirect header so htmx reloads the correct view without a full-page reload.

tour.title: Key Views
tour.description: Views under html/includes/views/ are PHP partials rendered by the view router from Step 4. users_edit_form.php is the member detail sheet: it renders a tabbed interface (profile, accounting, follow-up notes, audit history) and is the primary editing surface for a member record. settings_general.php is the administrative settings hub, using a sidebar-plus-mobile-select navigation pattern and gating each tab behind the appropriate role check. The shared navigation chrome is provided by html/includes/partials/menu.php, which renders the sidebar with role-aware visibility so Reader-only users never see Writer or Manager sections.
tour.languageLesson: htmx partial rendering: when a user clicks a tab or nav link, htmx sends a GET request with the HX-Request: true header. index.php detects this header and returns only the view partial -- a few hundred lines of HTML -- rather than the full page layout. This achieves SPA-like speed without a JavaScript framework managing client-side routing or state.

tour.title: REST API Bootstrap & Routing
tour.description: The REST API lives under html/api/ and is a distinct subsystem from the htmx views. html/api/_bootstrap.php (fan-in 13, second only to bootstrap.php overall) is included by every API endpoint: it sets JSON response headers, opens the PDO connection, authenticates via session cookie or bearer token using the same auth library from Step 3, and exposes a send() helper that serialises responses. Clean URL routing (e.g. /api/members/42/groups) is handled by Apache rewrite rules in html/api/.htaccess, which map path segments to query parameters before PHP runs.
tour.languageLesson: Apache mod_rewrite in .htaccess translates clean REST paths (/api/members/42) into query-string form (members.php?id=42) before PHP executes. This keeps PHP files simple (they read $_GET['id']) while exposing a professional URL surface. The .htaccess file is effectively the routing table for the entire API and must be kept in sync with any new endpoint paths.

tour.title: REST API Endpoints
tour.description: Four files implement the bulk of the REST API surface. members.php (543 lines, fan-out 10) is the largest endpoint: paginated listing with search and virtual filter support (donor, retention cohorts), full CRUD, and segment membership queries. groups.php implements CRUD for segments and their membership lists with manager-level access control. compta.php handles accounting entries with date-range filtering and donation/contribution type distinction. suivi.php manages follow-up notes stored as user_properties rows. Together these files expose the full domain model from Step 5 as JSON consumed by the htmx frontend and by external integrations.

tour.title: API Reference & Architecture Docs
tour.description: doc/api.md is the complete REST API reference: authentication methods (session cookie or bearer token), the four roles (readonly/user/manager/admin) and what each permits, HTTP status codes, .htaccess routing conventions, and every endpoint with its parameters and response shape. doc/architecture.md goes deeper: it traces the full GET and POST request lifecycles from browser to database and back, explains each architectural layer, and describes the CSV import wizard state machine. Reading these after the code steps cements your understanding of the design decisions behind what you have seen.

tour.title: Infrastructure: Docker & CI
tour.description: The Docker stack is defined in docker-compose.yml (three services: php, mariadb, adminer) and the Dockerfile (php:8.2-apache base with pdo_mysql, pdftk-java for PDF attestation generation, and Apache modules rewrite/headers/brotli). The mariadb service mounts the schema from docker/initdb so the database is fully initialised on first start with no manual step. The CI pipeline is a GitHub Actions workflow that spins up the compose test stack, waits for the /login.php health check, installs Playwright, and runs the E2E test suite on every push to main and every pull request.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage builds are not used here because pdftk-java is a runtime dependency (not just a build tool) -- it is called at request time to generate PDF attestations. The Dockerfile installs it alongside PHP extensions in a single layer, which means the production image is larger than a minimal runtime image but avoids the complexity of a sidecar process.

project.name: MemberBase
project.description: PHP 8.2 web application for managing members, groups, accounting and donations. Used by Casa Alianza Suisse association.
